folio.label: 185v
folio.l: Ageyns Pompeus , fader thyn in lawe

note.notetxt: 594Cross in dry point in the right margin.ODR

folio.l: That of the Orient , had al the Chyualrie
As fer as æ the day , bigynnetú dawe
Thow thurgú thy knygúthode , hast he~ take & slawe
Saue fewe folk , that witú Pompeus fledde
Thurgú whicú æè puttest , al thorient in awe
Thanke fortune , that so wel the spedde
¢ But now a litel while , I wyl bywayle
This Pompeus , this noble gouänour
Of Rome , whicú æ fley at this bataille
I seye , oon of his men , a fals traitour
His hed of smot , to wynnen hi~ fauour
Of Iulius , and hi~ the hed he brougút
Allas Pompeie , of thorient Conquerour
That fortune , vn to swicú a fyn , the brougút
¢ To Rome a geyn , repairetú Iulius
Witú his téumphe , lauriat ful heye
But on a tyme , Brutus Cassius
That euäe hadde , of his hey estat , envye
Ful priuyly , hatú made , Conspiracie
Ageyns this Iulius , in sotile wyse
And cast the place , in whicú he shulde deye
Witú boydekyns , as I shal ©ow deuyse
¢ This Iulius , to the capitolie wente
Vp on a day , as he was wont to gon
And in the Capitolie , a noon hi~ hente
This false Brutus , & hise othere foon
And Styked hi~ , w Boydekyns , a noon
W manye a wounde , & thus they lete hi~ lye
But neuäe grunt he , at no stroke , but oon
Or elles at two , but if his storie lye
¢ So ma~ly was this Iulius , of herte
And so wel loued , estatly honeste
That thougú hise dedly woundes , so sore smerte
His mantel ouä hise hipes , caste he
For no man shulde sen , his pryuete
And as he lay of deyinge , in a traunce
And wist verreily , that ded was he
Of honeste , ©et had he , remembraunce
¢ Lucan to the , this storie I recomende
And to Swetou~ , and to valerius also
That of the storie , wryten word and ende
How æ theise grete , Conquerours two
